source_value_workbook: solar_wind_value_breakdown.xlsx / Summary + Solar_Map (reused from solar_hail; value ladder is peril-agnostic)
source_damage_artifact: NONE AVAILABLE — no solar-specific flood depth-damage artifact exists; provisional hazard axis uses generic industrial curves (JRC / HAZUS / USACE).
important_rule: This crosswalk normalizes and reconciles denominator/grain only; it is not external validation evidence. The flood failure unit and depth-damage function are provisional and NOT solar-calibrated.
pending_artifacts: Solar value workbook is reusable (peril-agnostic value ladder), but a solar-specific FLOOD depth-damage curve artifact does not yet exist.
Until a solar flood damage artifact is authored, the depth-damage function is proxied by a generic industrial curve (JRC North America Industrial) and must be labeled generic.

failure_unit.y_axis: electrical/BOS replacement damage ratio approximated by generic industrial depth-damage factor
failure_unit.note: Flood failure unit is electrical / balance-of-system (inverters, combiner/collection, cabling) and foundation scour — NOT PV module glass. This is the key difference from the hail pair, whose failure unit is PV_MODULE_GLASS_CELL.

flood_damage_grain.warning: The generic industrial curve is coarse and NOT solar-calibrated; it must not be used to tune a solar flood curve. It only bounds the plausible electrical/BOS failure-unit denominator.

INVERTER_SYSTEM.flood_vulnerability: high — power electronics fail on inundation unless elevated above design flood level

ELECTRICAL_COLLECTION.flood_vulnerability: high — combiner boxes, DC/AC cabling and connections not designed for sustained submersion (IEC 60529 ingress limits)

FOUNDATION.flood_vulnerability: medium — scour and washout above ~7 ft/s flow velocity (FM Global DS 7-106)

SUBSTATION.flood_vulnerability: high if not elevated — transformer/switchgear damage is a large-loss driver

PV_ARRAY.flood_vulnerability: low for standing modules — modules above flood depth are generally not the flood failure unit; contrast with hail where PV_ARRAY IS the failure unit

REPLACEMENT_FIELDWORK.flood_vulnerability: n/a — claim-time allocation bucket (cleanup, drying, re-termination, re-commissioning)

comparison_guidance: Flood direct damage should be compared to the FLOOD_ELECTRICAL_BOS bucket (inverters + electrical collection + foundation), NOT to PV_ARRAY module hardware.
Include SUBSTATION only when the loss narrative confirms transformer/switchgear inundation.
Include REPLACEMENT_FIELDWORK when the source includes cleanup / drying / re-termination / re-commissioning labor.

Do not compare BI/downtime, deductible/net-of-insurance, or premium effects to physical flood damage without a separate financial layer; most open solar flood dollars (First Solar/Zurich, South Alexander/Markel) are dominated by deductible and BI structure.
The depth-damage function is a generic industrial proxy and is NOT solar-calibrated; treat all depth→DR mappings as provisional until a solar flood damage artifact exists.
